ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stores" (
  "store_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each store */,
  "store_name" TEXT /* Name of the store */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the store */,
  "store_size" REAL /* Size of the store in square feet */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store manager */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the store */,
  "store_type" TEXT /* Type of store (e.g., supermarket, boutique) */,
  "opening_date" TEXT /* Date the store opened */,
  "last_renovation_date" TEXT /* Date of the last renovation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("store_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stores_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales" (
  "sales_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales entry */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the sales data */,
  "weekly_sales" REAL /* Weekly sales for the store */,
  "holiday_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the day is a holiday (0/1) */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "quantity_sold" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product sold */,
  "unit_price" REAL /* Unit price of the product */,
  "total_sales" REAL /* Total sales amount for the product */,
  "promotion_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether there was a promotion (0/1) */,
  "return_amount" REAL /* Amount of returns on that day */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sales_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather entry */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather data */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature on that date */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation on that date */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed on that date */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity level on that date */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Visibility on that date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fuel_prices" (
  "fuel_price_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fuel price entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the fuel price data */,
  "fuel_price" REAL /* Fuel price on that date */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region for which the fuel price is applicable */,
  "fuel_type" TEXT /* Type of fuel (e.g., gasoline, diesel) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fuel_price_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpi" (
  "cpi_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CPI entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the CPI data */,
  "cpi" REAL /* CPI value on that date */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region for which the CPI is applicable */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of goods (e.g., food, housing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cpi_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "unemployment" (
  "unemployment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unemployment entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the unemployment data */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate on that date */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region for which the unemployment rate is applicable */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group for the unemployment rate */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender for the unemployment rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unemployment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast entry */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the forecasted sales data */,
  "forecasted_sales" REAL /* Forecasted sales for the store on that date */,
  "forecast_method" TEXT /* Method used for the forecast (e.g., ARIMA, LSTM) */,
  "forecast_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the forecast */,
  "confidence_interval" TEXT /* Confidence interval for the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., store manager, analyst, admin) */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store the user is associated with */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id")
)
